NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. LORD, You have been favorable to Your land; You have brought back the captivity of Jacob.
NKJV.2: You have forgiven the iniquity of Your people; You have covered all their sin. Selah
NKJV.3: You have taken away all Your wrath; You have turned from the fierceness of Your anger.
NKJV.4: Restore us, O God of our salvation, And cause Your anger toward us to cease.
NKJV.5: Will You be angry with us forever? Will You prolong Your anger to all generations?
NKJV.6: Will You not revive us again, That Your people may rejoice in You?
NKJV.7: Show us Your mercy, LORD, And grant us Your salvation.
NKJV.8: I will hear what God the LORD will speak, For He will speak peace To His people and to His saints; But let them not turn back to folly.
NKJV.9: Surely His salvation is near to those who fear Him, That glory may dwell in our land.
NKJV.10: Mercy and truth have met together;  Righteousness and peace have kissed.
NKJV.11: Truth shall spring out of the earth, And righteousness shall look down from heaven.
NKJV.12: Yes, the LORD will give what is good; And our land will yield its increase.
NKJV.13: Righteousness will go before Him, And shall make His footsteps our pathway.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S>, A Psalm<S>4210</S> for the sons<S>1121</S> of Korah<S>7141</S>. LORD<S>3068</S>, thou hast been favourable<S>7521</S> unto thy land<S>776</S>: thou hast brought back<S>7725</S> the captivity<S>7622</S> <S>7622</S> of Jacob<S>3290</S>. <sup>for the sons: or, of the sons</sup> <sup>favourable: or, well pleased</sup>

KJV.4: Turn<S>7725</S> us, O God<S>430</S> of our salvation<S>3468</S>, and cause thine anger<S>3708</S> toward us to cease<S>6565</S>.
KJV.5: Wilt thou be angry<S>599</S> with us for ever<S>5769</S>? wilt thou draw out<S>4900</S> thine anger<S>639</S> to all<S>1755</S> generations<S>1755</S>?
KJV.6: Wilt thou not revive<S>2421</S> us again<S>7725</S>: that thy people<S>5971</S> may rejoice<S>8055</S> in thee?
KJV.7: Shew<S>7200</S> us thy mercy<S>2617</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, and grant<S>5414</S> us thy salvation<S>3468</S>.
KJV.8: I will hear<S>8085</S> what God<S>410</S> the LORD<S>3068</S> will speak<S>1696</S>: for he will speak<S>1696</S> peace<S>7965</S> unto his people<S>5971</S>, and to his saints<S>2623</S>: but let them not turn again<S>7725</S> to folly<S>3690</S>.
KJV.9: Surely his salvation<S>3468</S> is nigh<S>7138</S> them that fear<S>3373</S> him; that glory<S>3519</S> may dwell<S>7931</S> in our land<S>776</S>.

KJV.12: Yea, the LORD<S>3068</S> shall give<S>5414</S> that which is good<S>2896</S>; and our land<S>776</S> shall yield<S>5414</S> her increase<S>2981</S>.
KJV.13: Righteousness<S>6664</S> shall go<S>1980</S> before<S>6440</S> him; and shall set<S>7760</S> us in the way<S>1870</S> of his steps<S>6471</S>.

ESV.1: LORD, you were favorable to your land; you restored the fortunes of Jacob.
ESV.2: You forgave the iniquity of your people; you covered all their sin. Selah
ESV.3: You withdrew all your wrath; you turned from your hot anger.
ESV.4: Restore us again, O God of our salvation, and put away your indignation toward us!
ESV.5: Will you be angry with us forever? Will you prolong your anger to all generations?
ESV.6: Will you not revive us again, that your people may rejoice in you?
ESV.7: Show us your steadfast love, O LORD, and grant us your salvation.
ESV.8: Let me hear what God the LORD will speak, for he will speak peace to his people, to his saints; but let them not turn back to folly.
ESV.9: Surely his salvation is near to those who fear him, that glory may dwell in our land.
ESV.10: Steadfast love and faithfulness meet; righteousness and peace kiss each other.
ESV.11: Faithfulness springs up from the ground, and righteousness looks down from the sky.
ESV.12: Yes, the LORD will give what is good, and our land will yield its increase.
ESV.13: Righteousness will go before him and make his footsteps a way.

NASB.1: For the choir director. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. O LORD, You showed favor to Your land; You restored the captivity of Jacob.
NASB.2: You forgave the iniquity of Your people; You covered all their sin. Selah.
NASB.3: You withdrew all Your fury; You turned away from Your burning anger.
NASB.4: Restore us, O God of our salvation, And cause Your indignation toward us to cease.
NASB.5: Will You be angry with us forever? Will You prolong Your anger to all generations?
NASB.6: Will You not Yourself revive us again, That Your people may rejoice in You?
NASB.7: Show us Your lovingkindness, O LORD, And grant us Your salvation.
NASB.8: I will hear what God the LORD will say; For He will speak peace to His people, to His godly ones; But let them not turn back to folly.
NASB.9: Surely His salvation is near to those who fear Him, That glory may dwell in our land.
NASB.10: Lovingkindness and truth have met together; Righteousness and peace have kissed each other.
NASB.11: Truth springs from the earth, And righteousness looks down from heaven.
NASB.12: Indeed, the LORD will give what is good, And our land will yield its produce.
NASB.13: Righteousness will go before Him And will make His footsteps into a way.

NLT.1:
LORD, you poured out blessings on your land!
You restored the fortunes of Israel.
NLT.2:
You forgave the guilt of your people —
yes, you covered all their sins. Interlude
NLT.3:
You held back your fury.
You kept back your blazing anger.
NLT.4:
Now restore us again, O God of our salvation.
Put aside your anger against us once more.
NLT.5:
Will you be angry with us always?
Will you prolong your wrath to all generations?
NLT.6:
Won’t you revive us again,
so your people can rejoice in you?
NLT.7:
Show us your unfailing love, O LORD,
and grant us your salvation.
NLT.8:
I listen carefully to what God the LORD is saying,
for he speaks peace to his faithful people.
But let them not return to their foolish ways.
NLT.9:
Surely his salvation is near to those who fear him,
so our land will be filled with his glory.
NLT.10:
Unfailing love and truth have met together.
Righteousness and peace have kissed!
NLT.11:
Truth springs up from the earth,
and righteousness smiles down from heaven.
NLT.12:
Yes, the LORD pours down his blessings.
Our land will yield its bountiful harvest.
NLT.13:
Righteousness goes as a herald before him,
preparing the way for his steps.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by the sons of Korah. Yahweh, you have been favorable to your land. You have restored the fortunes of Jacob.
WEB.2: You have forgiven the iniquity of your people. You have covered all their sin. Selah.
WEB.3: You have taken away all your wrath. You have turned from the fierceness of your anger.
WEB.4: Turn us, God of our salvation, and cause your indignation toward us to cease.
WEB.5: Will you be angry with us forever? Will you draw out your anger to all generations?
WEB.6: Won’t you revive us again, that your people may rejoice in you?
WEB.7: Show us your loving kindness, Yahweh. Grant us your salvation.
WEB.8: I will hear what God, Yahweh, will speak, for he will speak peace to his people, his saints; but let them not turn again to folly.
WEB.9: Surely his salvation is near those who fear him, that glory may dwell in our land.
WEB.10: Mercy and truth meet together. Righteousness and peace have kissed each other.
WEB.11: Truth springs out of the earth. Righteousness has looked down from heaven.
WEB.12: Yes, Yahweh will give that which is good. Our land will yield its increase.
WEB.13: Righteousness goes before him, And prepares the way for his steps.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director. A psalm of the sons of Korah. [1] LORD, you showed favor to your land; ⓘ
you restored the fortunes of Jacob. [1] ⓙ
CSB17.2:
You forgave your people’s guilt;
you covered all their sin. ⓚ Selah
CSB17.3:
You withdrew all your fury;
you turned from your burning anger. ⓛ
CSB17.4:
Return to us, God of our salvation,
and abandon your displeasure with us. ⓜ
CSB17.5:
Will you be angry with us forever?
Will you prolong your anger for all generations? ⓝ
CSB17.6:
Will you not revive us again
so that your people may rejoice in you? ⓐ
CSB17.7:
Show us your faithful love, LORD,
and give us your salvation. ⓑ
CSB17.8:
I will listen to what God will say;
surely the LORD will declare peace
to his people, his faithful ones,
and not let them go back to foolish ways. ⓒ
CSB17.9:
His salvation is very near those who fear him,
so that glory may dwell in our land. ⓓ
CSB17.10:
Faithful love and truth will join together;
righteousness and peace will embrace. ⓔ
CSB17.11:
Truth will spring up from the earth,
and righteousness will look down from heaven. ⓕ
CSB17.12:
Also, the LORD will provide what is good,
and our land will yield its crops. ⓖ
CSB17.13:
Righteousness will go before him
to prepare the way for his steps. ⓗ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. O LORD, You have [at last] shown favor to Your land [of Canaan];
You have restored [from Babylon] the captives of Jacob (Israel).
AMP.2:
You have forgiven the wickedness of Your people;
You have covered all their sin. Selah.
AMP.3:
You have withdrawn all Your wrath,
You have turned away from Your burning anger.
AMP.4:
Restore us, O God of our salvation,
And cause Your indignation toward us to cease.
AMP.5:
Will You be angry with us forever?
Will You prolong Your anger to all generations?
AMP.6:
Will You not revive us and bring us to life again,
That Your people may rejoice in You?
AMP.7:
Show us Your lovingkindness, O LORD,
And grant us Your salvation.
AMP.8:
I will hear [with expectant hope] what God the LORD will say,
For He will speak peace to His people, to His godly ones—
But let them not turn again to folly.
AMP.9:
Surely His salvation is near to those who [reverently] fear Him [and obey Him with submissive wonder],
That glory [the manifest presence of God] may dwell in our land.
AMP.10:
Steadfast love and truth and faithfulness meet together;
Righteousness and peace kiss each other.
AMP.11:
Truth springs from the earth,
And righteousness looks down from heaven.
AMP.12:
Indeed, the LORD will give what is good,
And our land will yield its produce.
AMP.13:
Righteousness will go before Him
And will make His footsteps into a way [in which to walk].

MSG.1:
A Korah psalm
God, you smiled on your good earth!
You brought good times back to Jacob!
MSG.2:
You lifted the cloud of guilt from your people,
you put their sins far out of sight.
MSG.3:
You took back your sin-provoked threats,
you cooled your hot, righteous anger.
MSG.4:
Help us again, God of our help;
don't hold a grudge against us forever.
MSG.5:
You aren't going to keep this up, are you?
scowling and angry, year after year?
MSG.6:
Why not help us make a fresh start—a resurrection life?
Then your people will laugh and sing!
MSG.7:
Show us how much you love us, God!
Give us the salvation we need!
MSG.8:
I can't wait to hear what he'll say.
God's about to pronounce his people well,
The holy people he loves so much,
so they'll never again live like fools.
MSG.9:
See how close his salvation is to those who fear him?
Our country is home base for Glory!
MSG.10:
Love and Truth meet in the street,
Right Living and Whole Living embrace and kiss!
MSG.11:
Truth sprouts green from the ground,
Right Living pours down from the skies!
MSG.12:
Oh yes! God gives Goodness and Beauty;
our land responds with Bounty and Blessing.
MSG.13:
Right Living strides out before him,
and clears a path for his passage.

NIV.1:
Psalm 85
For the director of music. Of the Sons of Korah. A psalm.
You showed favor to your land, O Lord;
you restored the fortunes of Jacob.
NIV.2:
You forgave the iniquity of your people
and covered all their sins. Selah
NIV.3:
You set aside all your wrath
and turned from your fierce anger.
NIV.4:
Restore us again, O God our Savior,
and put away your displeasure toward us.
NIV.5:
Will you be angry with us forever?
Will you prolong your anger through all generations?
NIV.6:
Will you not revive us again,
that your people may rejoice in you?
NIV.7:
Show us your unfailing love, O Lord,
and grant us your salvation.
NIV.8:
I will listen to what God the Lord will say;
he promises peace to his people, his saints—
but let them not return to folly.
NIV.9:
Surely his salvation is near those who fear him,
that his glory may dwell in our land.
NIV.10:
Love and faithfulness meet together;
righteousness and peace kiss each other.
NIV.11:
Faithfulness springs forth from the earth,
and righteousness looks down from heaven.
NIV.12:
The Lord will indeed give what is good,
and our land will yield its harvest.
NIV.13:
Righteousness goes before him
and prepares the way for his steps.

NRSVCE.1:
LORD, you were favorable to your land;
you restored the fortunes of Jacob.
NRSVCE.2:
You forgave the iniquity of your people;
you pardoned all their sin. Selah
NRSVCE.3:
You withdrew all your wrath;
you turned from your hot anger.
NRSVCE.4:
Restore us again, O God of our salvation,
and put away your indignation toward us.
NRSVCE.5:
Will you be angry with us forever?
Will you prolong your anger to all generations?
NRSVCE.6:
Will you not revive us again,
so that your people may rejoice in you?
NRSVCE.7:
Show us your steadfast love, O LORD,
and grant us your salvation.
NRSVCE.8:
Let me hear what God the LORD will speak,
for he will speak peace to his people,
to his faithful, to those who turn to him in their hearts.
NRSVCE.9:
Surely his salvation is at hand for those who fear him,
that his glory may dwell in our land.
NRSVCE.10:
Steadfast love and faithfulness will meet;
righteousness and peace will kiss each other.
NRSVCE.11:
Faithfulness will spring up from the ground,
and righteousness will look down from the sky.
NRSVCE.12:
The LORD will give what is good,
and our land will yield its increase.
NRSVCE.13:
Righteousness will go before him,
and will make a path for his steps.

NET.1: O LORD, you showed favor to your land; you restored the well-being of Jacob.
NET.2: You pardoned the wrongdoing of your people; you forgave all their sin. (Selah)
NET.3: You withdrew all your fury; you turned back from your raging anger.
NET.4: Restore us, O God our deliverer! Do not be displeased with us!
NET.5: Will you stay mad at us forever? Will you remain angry throughout future generations?
NET.6: Will you not revive us once more? Then your people will rejoice in you!
NET.7: O LORD, show us your loyal love! Bestow on us your deliverance!
NET.8: I will listen to what God the LORD says. For he will make peace with his people, his faithful followers. Yet they must not return to their foolish ways.
NET.9: Certainly his loyal followers will soon experience his deliverance; then his splendor will again appear in our land.
NET.10: Loyal love and faithfulness meet; deliverance and peace greet each other with a kiss.
NET.11: Faithfulness grows from the ground, and deliverance looks down from the sky.
NET.12: Yes, the LORD will bestow his good blessings, and our land will yield its crops.
NET.13: Deliverance goes before him, and prepares a pathway for him.
